name: Aberdeen Group Semiconductor Research Practice Definition (2002)
description: Formal definition of Aberdeen Group's Semiconductor Research Practice: focus on enabling semiconductor technologies for emerging digital markets (MEMS, photonics, software-defined radios, NPUs, UWB), a taxonomy of semiconductor ecosystem trends (design, fabrication, test), and detailed target account lists spanning 36+ firms (Agere, AMD, ARM, ATI, Broadcom, IBM, Intel, Nvidia, Samsung, Sony, TSMC, etc.). Captures the 2001 semiconductor downturn context (-32% to $132B WW revenue) and a forward-looking view of fabless trends, design/NRE cost inflation ($10M+ per device), 300mm facility cost ($2.7B), verification engineering dominance (70% of cycle), DFT, SoC test, and molecular/Plastic-Logic disruption candidates.
